headline: Nvidia to boost spending in Taiwan to $150 bn a year
summary: Nvidia will increase investment in Taiwan to $150 billion a year, the US chip giant's boss said Wednesday, describing the export-driven island as the "epicentre of the AI revolution"."Now, we're spending $100 going to $150 billion in Taiwan each year."

content.title: Nvidia to boost spending in Taiwan to $150 bn a year
content.summary: Nvidia will increase investment in Taiwan to $150 billion a year, the US chip giant's boss said Wednesday, describing the export-driven island as the "epicentre of the AI revolution"."Now, we're spending $100 going to $150 billion in Taiwan each year."

headline: Value of South Korean chip giant SK hynix tops $1 tn
summary: The market value of South Korean memory chip maker SK hynix soared past $1 trillion on Wednesday thanks to frenzied global demand for the computing hardware that powers artificial intelligence tools.Shares in SK hynix, which supplies Silicon Valley's AI chip titan Nvidia with advanced high-bandwidth memory, were up more than 11 percent in early afternoon trade.

headline: Nvidia to spend $150 billion a year in Taiwan, 'epicentre' of AI revolution, says CEO
summary: Nvidia's CEO said on Wednesday the chip company plans to invest around $150 ‌billion a year in Taiwan, terming it the "epicentre" ‌of the AI revolution and predicting it will be the world's tech manufacturing ​hub for a long time.  Jensen ‌Huang, chief of ⁠the $5 trillion chipmaker, said.  Huang was speaking at a launch celebration in Taipei for the chip company's ⁠planned Taiwan headquarters, which he said will break ground this year and aims to become operational in 2030.

Jensen ‌Huang, chief of ⁠the $5 trillion chipmaker, said.  Huang was speaking at a launch celebration in Taipei for the chip company's ⁠planned Taiwan headquarters, which he said will break ground this year and aims to become operational in 2030.

headline: IREN’s US$1.6b Blackwell Bet Tests Power Advantage In AI Buildout
summary: IREN (NasdaqGS:IREN) agreed to purchase $1.6 billion of Nvidia Blackwell systems from Dell to power its AI data centers in Texas. The company plans to use this hardware to serve previously announced AI cloud contracts at its existing Texas campus. Management has highlighted power grid constraints as a key risk for new AI data centers, with many projects facing multi year delays. IREN points to its pre secured power infrastructure as a key factor in being able to deploy new AI compute...

headline: SK Hynix joins $1 trillion club after Samsung, Micron on AI chip boom
summary: SK Hynix topped $1 trillion in market value for the first time on Wednesday, joining its memory chip rivals Samsung Electronics and Micron Technology in reaching the milestone on an AI-driven rally.  Shares of SK Hynix closed ‌the session up 9.3%, after rising as much as 14.9% during the session to take the South Korean chipmaker's market value to a record ‌1,680 trillion won ($1.12 trillion) and propel the country's benchmark KOSPI index to a record high.  Domestic rival Samsung surpassed $1 trillion in market value for the first time on May 6, while U.S.-listed Micron did ​so on Tuesday.

content.title: SK Hynix joins $1 trillion club after Samsung, Micron on AI chip boom
content.summary: SK Hynix topped $1 trillion in market value for the first time on Wednesday, joining its memory chip rivals Samsung Electronics and Micron Technology in reaching the milestone on an AI-driven rally.  Shares of SK Hynix closed ‌the session up 9.3%, after rising as much as 14.9% during the session to take the South Korean chipmaker's market value to a record ‌1,680 trillion won ($1.12 trillion) and propel the country's benchmark KOSPI index to a record high.  Domestic rival Samsung surpassed $1 trillion in market value for the first time on May 6, while U.S.-listed Micron did ​so on Tuesday.
